articles.title: PowerLutions Solar Completes Rooftop Solar Project at AvalonBay's Boonton Community
articles.summary: PowerLutions Solar has completed a 747 kW DC multi-interconnection rooftop solar project at AvalonBay's community in Boonton, New Jersey, in partnership with REV Energy Ventures. The system generated approximately 821 MWh in its first year, offsetting a significant portion of common-area electricity use and avoiding an estimated 300 metric tons of CO2 emissions annually. This initiative highlights AvalonBay's commitment to sustainability and demonstrates how strategic clean energy partnerships can lead to long-term energy savings and environmental benefits.

Siemens taps Jabil to expand electrical equipment manufacturing in Virginia
articles.summary: Siemens is partnering with Jabil to open a new 300,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Prince George County, Virginia, to expand U.S. production of critical medium-voltage switchgear and integrated power distribution solutions. This $30 million investment aims to accelerate speed to power for data center, utility, and industrial customers, create over 350 new jobs, and strengthen the domestic supply chain. The facility is slated to begin production in Fall 2026, addressing the growing demand for power infrastructure driven by data center expansion and electrification.
articles.content: Siemens is partnering with Jabil to open a new 300,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Prince George County, Virginia, to expand U.S. production of critical medium-voltage switchgear and integrated power distribution solutions. This $30 million investment aims to accelerate speed to power for data center, utility, and industrial customers, create over 350 new jobs, and strengthen the domestic supply chain. The facility is slated to begin production in Fall 2026, addressing the growing demand for power infrastructure driven by data center expansion and electrification.

HP Inc earnings in focus: Can AI PCs offset tariff pressure?
articles.summary: HP Inc. is set to announce its fiscal second-quarter earnings, with focus on how emerging AI-driven PC demand will balance against increased cost pressures from trade regulations. Analysts anticipate Q2 earnings of $0.71 per share on $13.99 billion in revenue, representing a 6% year-over-year revenue increase but a sequential decline. Investors will closely watch Personal Systems revenue and margins, updated full-year guidance regarding tariff impacts, and commentary on the ongoing leadership transition as the company operates under an interim CEO.
articles.content: HP Inc. is set to announce its fiscal second-quarter earnings, with focus on how emerging AI-driven PC demand will balance against increased cost pressures from trade regulations. Analysts anticipate Q2 earnings of $0.71 per share on $13.99 billion in revenue, representing a 6% year-over-year revenue increase but a sequential decline. Investors will closely watch Personal Systems revenue and margins, updated full-year guidance regarding tariff impacts, and commentary on the ongoing leadership transition as the company operates under an interim CEO.

Chevron shareholders reject proposal that requires independent board chair separate from CEO role
articles.summary: Chevron shareholders have rejected a proposal to separate the roles of independent board chair and CEO, aligning with the company's recommendation. The proposal, brought by the National Legal and Policy Center, was opposed by Chevron, which argued for flexibility in board structure, despite proxy advisory firm Glass Lewis recommending its approval. This decision follows a similar rejection by ConocoPhillips investors earlier in May.
articles.content: Chevron shareholders have rejected a proposal to separate the roles of independent board chair and CEO, aligning with the company's recommendation. This decision follows a similar rejection by ConocoPhillips investors earlier in May.
